title: Nursery Rhyme. CXXX. Songs.
content:
Oh, where are you going,
            My pretty maiden fair,
        With your red rosy cheeks,
            And your coal-black hair?
        I'm going a-milking,
            Kind sir, says she;
        And it's dabbling in the dew,
            Where you'll find me.

        May I go with you,
            My pretty maiden fair, &c.
        Oh, you may go with me,
            Kind sir, says she, &c.

        If I should chance to kiss you,
            My pretty maiden fair, &c.
        The wind may take it off again,
            Kind sir, says she, &c.

        And what is your father,
            My pretty maiden fair, &c.
        My father is a farmer,
            Kind sir, says she, &c.

        And what is your mother,
            My pretty maiden fair, &c.
        My mother is a dairy-maid,
            Kind sir, says she, &c.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Unknown delivers a powerful performance in "Nursery Rhyme. CXXX. Songs."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
